page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V1 model running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in PassMark memory testing and strong performance in multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation tasks. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering strong read and write speeds across both small cached and large uncached blocks. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, also rank in the strong tier, alongside static web serving throughput. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations generally align with average benchmark tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7gd.12xlarge leverages the ARM-based Graviton architecture to offer a high resource density and favorable performance-per-watt profile compared to traditional x86 architectures. The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage eliminates the need for external block storage for high-throughput local operations, though it presents a tradeoff for workloads requiring persistent storage across instance terminations. This server is highly suited for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive general-purpose applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. The server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory scores, strong uncached memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core execution, particularly in software compilation, database operations, and Redis SET transactions. While single-core performance and cryptographic operations remain in the average tier, its ARM-based architecture provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads. The m7gd.12xlarge is optimized for database hosting, static web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.12xlarge is an arm64 server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose server featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and exceptional memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores on arm64 architecture.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
Provides 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for database hosting, Redis operations, and multi-threaded software compilation.
